rulings.Case Title: GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC VS. BRADDOCK ROAD, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdraw And Amend Admission
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday , March 17, 2025 Line 1. 3 - DEFENDANT FIRENZE GROUP, LLC.'s Motion To Withdraw And Amend Admission. All three of defendants/cross-complainants' motions to withdraw their admission that the Guaranty is enforceable are denied. The moving parties fail to provide any evidentiary support for a mistake that is relievable by CCP 2033.330, much less a mistake that is excusable. Moreover, plaintiff/cross-defendants have shown that they will suffer significant prejudice by the granting of these motions due to the impending discovery cutoff and trial dates and moving parties' inability and/or unwillingness to provide any evidence or discovery responses for their contention that they were fraudulently induced to enter into the Guaranty. Opposing parties' request to file sur-reply papers is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC VS. BRADDOCK ROAD, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday , March 17, 2025 Line 1. 1 - CROSS DEFENDANTs GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC, MARK JORGENSEN,and STEVEN KAY DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendants' demurrer to the sole cause of action in the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-complainants' admission that the Guaranty is enforceable precludes their claim that they were fraudulently induced to enter into the Guaranty as a matter of law. Cross-complainants fail to provide any basis to believe that they are able to remediate this deficiency and thus there is no reason to give them an opportunity to file an amended complaint at this late stage of this cae. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: MISAEL HERNANDEZ VS. RECOLOGY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Medical Records And Authorizations; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday , March 17, 2025 Line 8. DEFENDANT RECOLOGY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Production Of Medical Records And Authorizations; And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Recology's motion to compel production of medical records and authorizations is granted and its request for monetary sanctions is granted in part. The discovery sought by Recology is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff Misael Hernandez's objection to the original deposition raised privacy, yet plaintiff attributes the suicide attempt to alleged mistreatment while he was working for defendant. The sought records are therefore directly relevant to this case and should be disclosed. (Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 842 ["An implicit waiver of a party's constitutional rights encompassesdiscovery directly relevant to plaintiff's claims and essential to a fair resolution of the lawsuit"].) Plaintiff testified that he stayed at Pike's Peak and was treated by a company called Wagner. (Sheldon Decl. 9, Exh. B [Plaintiff's Depo., at 110:9-11; 112:14-113:2.]) The record shows that these documents have not been disclosed. (Sheldon Reply Decl., par. 4.) No later than March 31, 2025 plaintiff is required to produce and/or sign authorizations for the mental health records related to plaintiff's hospitalization in a mental institute, any records relating to his more than four months of weekly mental health treatment, and the bills and expenses he claims to have incurred from his medical providers. While plaintiff lacked substantial justification for his piecemeal and incomplete disclosures and should have executed the authorizations earlier since the documents were in his "control" (CCP 2031.010), the amount of monetary sanctions sought by defendant is excessive Plaintiff and his counsel, The Law Offices of Daniel Feder, jointly and severally shall pay $5,000 as reasonable sanctions to defendant by April 30, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA OSTROVSKY VS. LEGALMATCH.COM, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday , March 17, 2025 Line 9. DEFENDANT DMITRY SHUBOV, VLADIMIR SHUBOV,and LEGALMATCH.COM MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Defendants' motion to quash the subpoena served on Payward and for a protective order is continued to March 20, 2025 to give the parties a further opportunity to meet and confer in an effort to reach an agreement limiting the documents sought to the transaction(s) alleged to be wrongful in the second amended complaint and to provide for confidentiality of any private financial information in those documents. As written, the document requests go well beyond the allegations in the second amended complaint. As the plaintiff apparently realizes, the requests need to be confined to what is alleged in that complaint. The parties are required to engage in a further meet and confer session either in-person or by remote platform no later than the end of the day on March 18, 2025 in a good faith effort to reach agreement on all issues raised by this motion. If they are unsuccessful in resolving all issues raised by this motion, each side must send an email to department 302's email box established for the contesting of tentative rulings by 4pm on March 19, 2025 stating succinctly and without argument her or their proposal for limiting the documents sought to the transaction(s) alleged to be wrongful in the second amended complaint and to provide for confidentiality of any private financial information in those documents. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGIE MILON VS. JOHN MUNGUIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday , March 17, 2025 Line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT GEORGE BENETATOS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant George Benetatos' demurrer to all causes of action alleged against him in the third amended cross-complaint filed by John Mungia and Trad'r Sam is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. The third amended cross-complaint fails to allege any explanation grounded in the evidence why the attorney-client civil conspiracy allegations in the second amended cross-complaint were omitted from the third amended cross-complaint. Nor does the third amended complaint provide any explanation for the alleging of the new allegations against Mr. Benetatos which are inconsistent with the allegations of the second amended cross-complaint. This court's ruling on the demurrer to the second amended cross-complaint did not relieve cross-complainants of the obligation to provide adequate explanations for the omission of the conspiracy allegations and the addition of allegations that are inconsistent with previously made allegations. The court concludes that as to the claims alleged against Mr. Benetatos the third amended cross-complaint is a sham pleading and an ill-disguised effort to avoid the requirements of Civil Code 1714.10(a). Because cross-complainants have not provided any evidence to show that they are able to allege adequate explanations for their omission of the conspiracy allegations and the addition of the new allegations, there is no basis for granting them leave to file a fourth amended cross-complaint. While the court admonishes Mr. Bentetatos for his failure to adhere to the meet and confer requirements for the filing of a demurrer, under the circumstances of this case the court exercises its discretion to decide the demurrer on its merits. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: CROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. LAKESHA BARBARIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Standing And Compel Private Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday , March 17, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANT LAKESHA BARBARIN's Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Standing And Compel Private Arbitration. Off calendar without prejudice to being re-filed. Per Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b), this motion, which may have been served by mail outside the State of California, was required to be served 16 court days plus 10 calendar days prior to the hearing, The certificate of service stating that service was by mail and/or electronic service is insufficient for the court to determine that any lesser time for service is permissible. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
